seoTitle: How to Add a WhatsApp Button to a Canva Site
metaDescription: Learn how to add a WhatsApp button to your Canva website so visitors can message you directly for quick, real-time support.
intro: A WhatsApp button on your website gives visitors an instant way to reach out, which is especially useful for small businesses handling customer questions or bookings. This guide covers adding that button to a Canva-built website.

steps.heading: Open your Canva website project
steps.body: Go into the website editor for the site where you want to add the button.

steps.heading: Search for a WhatsApp element or button
steps.body: Look in Elements or the app integrations panel for a WhatsApp button component.

steps.heading: Add your WhatsApp number
steps.body: Enter the phone number you want customer messages to be sent to.

steps.heading: Position the button on your page
steps.body: Place it somewhere visible, such as the header, footer, or a floating position on the page.

steps.heading: Test the button before publishing
steps.body: Click the button in preview mode to confirm it opens a WhatsApp chat correctly.

faq.question: Do visitors need WhatsApp installed for the button to work?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking the button opens WhatsApp Web or the app, so visitors need an active WhatsApp account to send a message.

faq.question: Can I customize the button's text or color?
faq.answer: Depending on the element used, you can often adjust the button's label and color to match your site's branding.

faq.question: Is adding a WhatsApp button free in Canva?
faq.answer: Basic button elements are typically available on the free plan, though some integrations may require Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I use a different messaging app instead?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create a similar button linking to other messaging platforms using the same link-based approach.
